government.title: Government Integrations
government.desc: myGov, SİMA auth, and SİMA digital signing — built into your product.

hero.title: myGov, SİMA auth, and SİMA digital signing — built into your product
hero.lede: We connect your platform to Azerbaijan’s identity and e-signature infrastructure. Verified citizens, legally binding signatures, fewer dropouts at onboarding.

problem.title: Government integrations are where products quietly break
problem.p1: Sandbox docs are partial. Production certificates expire mid-launch. Edge cases in citizen data show up only at scale, after compliance has already signed off.
problem.p2: Most teams ship a working demo and hit walls later: brittle session handling, signature flows that lock users out, audit trails regulators don’t accept.
problem.p3: The cost is not just engineering time. It’s onboarding drop-off, failed signings, and a product that can’t pass an audit.

solution.title: Production-tested integrations, not demos
solution.p1: We integrate myGov, SİMA authentication, and SİMA digital signing the way they need to behave in production: with retries, fallbacks, error states users can act on, and audit trails regulators accept.
solution.p2: Every flow is built locale-first, mobile-first, and tested against the real sandbox before we go near a production certificate.

bullets.b1: myGov SSO with session handling that survives token expiry and network hiccups
bullets.b2: SİMA auth flows tuned for first-time users, not just power users
bullets.b3: Legally binding SİMA digital signing with verifiable audit logs
bullets.b4: Locale-aware UX in Azerbaijani, English, and Russian
bullets.b5: Clean handover: certificates, runbooks, and monitoring you actually own

solution.visualAlt: Diagram showing identity and signature flow between a product and Azerbaijani government services

uc1.title: Fintech KYC onboarding
uc1.description: Verify citizens in seconds with myGov or SİMA, replace manual document checks, and cut drop-off at sign-up.

uc2.title: Digital contract signing
uc2.description: Replace wet ink with SİMA digital signatures — legally binding, audit-ready, accepted by regulators.

uc3.title: Citizen portal SSO
uc3.description: Let residents sign in with their existing myGov identity. No new accounts, no forgotten passwords.

uc4.title: Document workflow automation
uc4.description: Route, sign, and archive regulated documents end-to-end with verifiable signatures and immutable history.

howItWorks.title: From scoping to a signed-off production cutover
howItWorks.footnote: Typical engagement: 4–8 weeks to production, depending on the scope and certificate timeline.

s1.label: Discovery and threat model
s1.detail: We map your flows, data residency, and the exact gov services you need — then write the threat model.

s2.label: Sandbox integration
s2.detail: We build against the official sandbox, cover edge cases, and validate every state with your team.

s3.label: Production cutover
s3.detail: Certificate provisioning, staged rollout, and a full audit trail from day one.

s4.label: Monitoring and SLA
s4.detail: Live dashboards, on-call coverage, and a maintenance plan that keeps the integration current.

i1.title: Legally binding signatures
i1.description: SİMA signatures hold up in court and pass regulator review.

i2.title: Azerbaijan-grade compliance
i2.description: Built around local data residency, KVDB, and sector-specific rules from day one.

i3.title: Higher onboarding conversion
i3.description: Citizens sign in with an identity they already have. Fewer steps, fewer dropouts.

i4.title: No vendor lock-in
i4.description: You own the certificates, the runbooks, and the code. We hand it over clean.

q1.q: Which government services do you integrate?
q1.a: myGov (e-gov.az) single sign-on, SİMA authentication, and SİMA digital signing. If your product needs another Azerbaijani gov endpoint, we’ll scope it during discovery.

q2.q: How does the sandbox-to-production handoff work?
q2.a: We build and test in the official sandbox first, then provision production certificates with your team. Cutover is staged, monitored, and reversible. You get a runbook covering every step.

q3.q: What about data residency and KVDB compliance?
q3.a: Personal data stays in-country by default. We design storage, logging, and retention against KVDB requirements and your sector’s rules — banking, insurance, health, govtech. Audit trails are built to match.

q4.q: Do you maintain the integration after launch?
q4.a: Yes. Certificates expire, sandbox endpoints change, and edge cases surface at scale. We offer SLA-backed maintenance with monitoring, on-call coverage, and quarterly reviews.

q5.q: Can you integrate with our existing systems?
q5.a: Yes. We work alongside your backend, identity provider, and document stack — REST, SOAP, message queues, whatever you run. The gov layer plugs into your product, not the other way around.

cta.title: Let’s scope your integration
cta.lede: Tell us which gov services you need and where you are today. We’ll come back with a plan within two business days.

about.title: About Lunatech
about.p1: Lunatech is a creative team focused on building and evolving digital products.
about.p2: We work at the intersection of design and technology to deliver functional, aesthetic, and lasting solutions for brands.
about.p3: Our goal isn’t just to exist online — it’s to make a difference.

approach.title: Our approach
approach.p1: We look at every project through a strategic lens.
approach.p2: We balance aesthetics and performance to ship products that create real user value.
approach.p3: For us, design isn’t only how it looks — it’s how it works.

process.title: Process
process.discoverDetail: Align on goals, constraints, and audiences before we shape the work.
process.designDetail: Structure, visuals, and interaction — crafted for clarity and conversion.
process.buildDetail: Engineering with polish: performance, accessibility, QA, and resilient delivery.
process.launchDetail: Ship, measure, and iterate with a clear handover and support plan.
process.subtitle: Each phase is planned and transparent.
